draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Design voice should feel conversational, use contractions, welcome humor, build trust through personality
facade.rejectImplies: Design voice should lean formal, authoritative, structured, maintain professional distance

evidence.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality
evidence.implication: Design voice should feel conversational, use contractions, welcome humor, build trust through personality

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer low-light interfaces for focus and reduced eye strain in AI tools
facade.label: Dark mode workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker palettes, high contrast text, subtle glows for UI elements
facade.rejectImplies: Explore lighter, brighter interfaces with more traditional white/neutral backgrounds

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer snappy, zero-latency interactions over animated transitions in an AI workspace where speed of thought matters
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize immediate visual feedback, direct clicks over gestures, and instant state changes over loading animations
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should embrace motion, micro-animations, and smooth transitions even if they add milliseconds of delay to interactions

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation paired with card-based content layout for an AI workspace, reducing cognitive load from tab switching
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. tabbed switching; card-based vs. list-based content organization
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore deeper: sidebar width, card density, card interactivity, and how cards respond to selection in sidebar
facade.rejectImplies: Test top navigation, full-width layouts, or list-based content structures instead

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer calming, gentle interfaces over sharp clarity—testing whether 'ai workspace' leans warm and organic rather than clinical.
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft focus blur
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace favors ambient, diffused aesthetics; may want breathing room and reduced cognitive load; leans toward organic/warm.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp, precise, high-contrast interfaces; values clarity and directness; leans toward cool/digital or clinical efficiency.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer seeing real-time data streams and KPIs in a glanceable dashboard format rather than reading narratives or static reports
facade.axisTargeted: Information density vs. real-time monitoring trade-off
facade.label: Live metrics dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: User values immediate feedback loops, live system performance visibility, and quantified productivity signals in their workspace interface
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers slower-burn context through prose, historical analysis, or deliberate check-ins rather than always-on metric feeds

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetics vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetics vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace may prefer professional, organized voice over casual warmth to establish credibility and clarity
facade.label: Structured and formal
facade.acceptImplies: AI workspace voice should use complete sentences, minimize contractions, maintain professional distance, emphasize clarity and precision over personality
facade.rejectImplies: Casual warmth is the stronger preference; users want approachability even in technical contexts

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
